evidence_packet.evidence_text: With its intricate stone carvings, grand clock tower and stained glass windows, Rochdale Town Hall is one of the UK's finest examples of Victorian Gothic architecture, and having just undergone a four-year renovation project, there’s never been a better time to see it Rochdale Pioneers Museum , Manchester 30 May 2026 View dates and times Date Time Session Features 30 May 2026 10:00 am-12:00 pm Additional information Always double check opening hours with the venue before making a special visit Incorporating the esplanade, Town Hall and Pioneers Museum , Rochdale has an incredible story to tell. And who better to tell it than Jonathan Schofield Tours, who’ll bring this tale to life as you explore the sites (and sights) of a fascinating town “Here all is completely different from Lancashire towns, and indeed all English towns. The town hall lies surrounded by public gardens on three sides and the church lies up a steep bank, and the bank is also a public garden. So the centre is green and pleasant.” Thus wrote the great guru of British architecture in the second half of the 20th century, Nikolaus Pevsner. On this tour you’ll assess the truth of that statement while peeling back the history of Rochdale, whose buildings and people come with some incredible stories The Town Hall really is an architectural gem. evidence_packet.evidence_text: A Lovely Weekend comedy festival Fairfield Social Club, Irk St, Manchester M4 4JT Fri 29th May, 2026 - Sun 31st May, 2026 A Lovely Weekend is back this spring, taking over Fairfield Social Club for three days of stand-up, live podcasts and some of the sharpest names on the UK comedy circuit Running from Friday 29 May to Sunday 31 May 2026, the festival packs around 20 shows into one weekend, bringing together established favourites, fast-rising names and cult comics for what’s shaping up to be one of the most stacked comedy bills in the city Friday kicks things off with Harriet Kemsley, fresh from winning over a whole new audience on Last One Laughing, alongside viral stand-up Tom Lawrinson, whose offbeat delivery and internet-born chaos have made him one of the most recognisable new voices in British comedy Saturday sees the return of Mark Silcox, who sold out at the festival last year and remains one of the strangest and funniest acts around, plus the first ever live edition of Sean McLoughlin’s podcast The Sean McTalk Show Then on Sunday, Sean McLoughlin is joined by stand-up favourite Josh Pugh, newly announced SNL UK cast member Larry Dean and acclaimed French comedian and writer Celya AB, rounding off the weekend with a line-up that feels properly loaded from start to finish

evidence_packet.evidence_text: Yan Tan Tethera Farmer’s Market at Track Track Brewing Co - Brewery & Taproom, Unit 18, Piccadilly Trading Estate, Manchester M1 2NP Sun 31st May, 2026 12pm - 8pm Yan Tan Tethera Farmer’s Market is back for its May edition, and this time it’s turning the dial up a bit On Sunday 31 May, the market returns as a full Food Festival edition, bringing together farmers, growers, chefs, drinks, music and a very strong line-up of Northern kitchens for a one-day celebration of the start of summer Already confirmed are Cafe San Juan, Erst, Freds, Stockport Provisions, Track Beer and Slice Culture Pizza, with fresh oysters, street food and more names still to be announced In proper Yan Tan Tethera style, each food vendor will be paired with one of the market’s produce stalls to create special one-off dishes built around what’s in season. Think chefs and growers side by side, swapping ingredients, cooking over fire, and leaning into a bit of creati The market takes its name from the old Northern shepherd’s counting system, a nod to rural life, seasonal rhythms and the people who work the land. What started as a small, thoughtful farmers’ market has grown into a proper meeting point for farmers, cooks, artists and the local

Inspired by the Deceptioncon robot costume currently on display in On The Line , which once stood on the picket line in support of workers’ rights Explore a century of struggles and stories of strength drawing on the museum’s nationally significant collections. Festival favourite Korea National Contemporary Dance Company (KNCDC) returns with a contrasting double bill which showcases the talent and precision of the company’s dancers, including the latest work from choreographer Young-doo Jung since his 2025 Olivier Award nomination for L In Jung ’s Voyage , a solitary spacecraft moves towards a singular point. Drawing inspiration from the Voyager deep-space probes launched in 1977 which dedicated a lifetime to venturing towards the unknown, Voyage attempts to pause the present moment and lead us into an uncharted Inspired by the repetitive actions of the traditional Japanese cup-and-ball toy ‘kendama’, Ryu Suzuki ’s Hakkō explores the gradual transformation of body and mind through focussed iterations of the simplest movements. The dancers enter a trance-like state of deep concentration, Korea’s only national contemporary dance company, KNCDC collaborates with artists of outstanding creative vision to create contemporary dance works that reflect history, society, and everyday life in modern Korea—resonating with audiences across regions and generations. In May 20 Young-doo Jung is a choreographer, director, dancer and actor who experiments with a wide range of forms and systems in choreography and composition, focusing on margins, breaths and subtle changes rather than excessive movement. This monthly event for trans and queer book lovers includes a guided writing session and book club discussion Meet and share work with like-minded people at The Grey Area. Each month, the group meets for a guided writing session, followed by a book club. You can join one or both sessions – and don't be put off coming if you didn't finish the book, you can still join in the discussion or The guided writing session runs from 6.30pm to 7.30pm. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Jonny Humphries North West GMP Keeley Aspinall, known as Kiki to her loved ones, was found dead at a house in Rochdale The family of a woman found dead at a house prompting a murder investigation said they would "always love and miss you" The body of Keeley Aspinall, 44, was found at the home in Tweedale Street, Rochdale, at about 16:00 BST on Monday after police were called to concerns for her welfare Greater Manchester Police said a man in his 30s had been arrested on suspicion of murder In a tribute, relatives said Keeley, known as Kiki, was "gone too soon" "We will always love you and miss you, love all your family xx." evidence_packet.evidence_text: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Brian Wheeler , Political reporter and Richard Wheeler , Political reporter PA Media Andy Burnham (left) and Wes Streeting are widely seen as two of Sir Keir Starmer's potential leadership rivals Andy Burnham and Wes Streeting have accused fo Sir Tony used a 5,600-word essay to argue the Labour government had "no coherent plan" for the country and had introduced policies that had held back business He urged Labour not to move to the left and to embrace the "radical centre" instead But Burnham and Streeting - both considered potential Labour leadership challengers to Sir Keir Starmer - suggested Sir Tony had overlooked how inequality is shaping modern politics Burnham, who is widely expected to challenge Sir Keir if he wins a by-election next month, told the Observer that Sir Tony "doesn't mention inequality once" in his essay "If you don't get how that's driving politics now, if you are not rooting your analysis in the fact that people are unable to live and that things that were taken for granted are no longer affordable, then you are not understanding what's going on," said the mayor of Greater Manc

evidence_packet.title: Kellogg's marks 88 years at Stretford site ahead of closure
evidence_packet.lead: The display has everything from vintage promotional materials to a plaque honouring a royal visit
evidence_packet.summary: The display has everything from vintage promotional materials to a plaque honouring a royal visit
evidence_packet.evidence_text: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker and Michelle Adamson , North West KELLANOVA Construction at the Trafford Park factory started in 1937 A global brand is marking almost 90 years at its Greater Manchester factory site ahead of closing its doors for good later th When the Kellogg's factory in Trafford Park was opened in 1938 it became the largest cereal factory in Europe. Now, almost nine decades on, the Stretford factory is set to close as manufacturing is transferred to their Wrexham site in north Wales Ahead of the closure, a free exhibition has been opened in Sale - with memorabilia from throughout the period celebrating the staff, products and culture behind the site "Kellogg's Trafford Park is one of the most iconic manufacturing sites in our city's and country's history," plant director Tim McKnight said The closure comes after Kellanova, which runs the cereal giant, announced plans to transfer manufacturing to its Wrexham site - due to the layout of the Trafford plant, which was said to be unsuitable for modern manufacturing, with a "large amount of redundant space" The exhibit - which is free to visit - is being hosted at the Trafford Local Studies and Archive Centre, in Sale

Credit: Greater Manchester Police A gang who tracked and targeted victim The three men - aged 33, 34 and 39 - were sentenced on 22 May 2026 to a combined total of over 21 years for a range of crimes including conspiracy to commit robbery and burglary Greater Manchester Police (GMP) started an investigation after a robbery in the car park of John Lewis in Cheadle, when a man reportedly threatened to stab a woman with a screwdriver if she did not give him her watch and engagement ring, worth nearly £200,000 combined Officers were able to track the mans car using CCTV, and investigations found that the victim had been followed using a tracker prior to the robbery Junior Owen, James Melia and Anthony Watson were later arrested, and a tracker, watches and drugs were seized from their addresses Following their arrests for the Cheadle robbery, officers discovered that they had committed further crimes targeting high-valued goods across a series burglaries across Cheshire and Greater Manchester

Armed police shot a suspect during the ordeal on Bury New Road last Friday (May 22) The 20-year-old, white British man, was arrested on suspicion of being concerned in the supply of Class A drugs. He was also taken to hospital with a suspected broken elbow, where he remains this morning (Wednesday), Greater Manchester Police says Officers went on to discover a gun and a 'significant' amount of Class A and Class B drugs at a property in Radcliffe . A 40-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of being concerned in supply of Class A and B, and possession of a firearm and ammunition following a raid by GMP's In an update issued this morning, GMP confirmed he had been released on bail. The car involved in the incident had failed to stop before being driven 'directly' at officers close to the M60 junction at Whitefield last Friday, prompting armed police to intervene Images showed a large cordon in place on Bury New Road, with forensics gathering evidence from the area. Fortunately, no officers or members of the public were injured

evidence_packet.evidence_text: News Greater Manchester News Stockport Detectives given longer to question Stockport murder suspects after mum shot dead Mum-of-one Shanice Brookes has now been identified as the woman killed in the incident News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 19:06, 27 May 202 Detectives have been given longer to question two murder suspects from Stockport after a mum-of-one was shot dead during a night out on Bank Holiday Monday Shanice Brookes has now been identified as the victim in the horror shooting, in which she was described by police as an 'innocent bystander' out celebrating. The 30-year-old mum-of-one suffered serious injuries and was later pronounced dead Police were called to the scene of the shooting at around 2.45am on Monday (May 25) outside One Four One bar on West Street. A major murder probe is ongoing to find those responsible for the fatal attack Three suspects were initially arrested on suspicion of murder in the hours following the shooting. evidence_packet.evidence_text: News Greater Manchester News Sheffield Detectives say 'someone knows who did this' after 'innocent woman enjoying night out' shot dead at bar Two suspects, who were arrested in Greater Manchester, remain in custody News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 18:03, 26 Detectives investigating the fatal shooting of a woman in Sheffield on Bank Holiday Monday have issued a new CCTV appeal, saying 'someone knows who did this' A major crime scene was put in place outside the One Four One bar on West Street in the centre of Sheffield in the early hours of Monday (May 25). The victim, a woman aged in her 30s, was found with serious injuries. Police say it's believed she was an 'innocent bystander enjoyin She was rushed to hospital where she later died, South Yorkshire Police confirmed. A murder investigation was launched as forensics scoured the scene Two men and a woman were swiftly arrested on suspicion of murder following the incident. One man, 30, and the woman, 32, were arrested near Stockport during the early hours of the morning, while the second man, aged 30, was arrested in Sheffield. It was confirmed on Tuesday that South Yorkshire Police have now issued a further appeal, along with a CCTV image showing a white Audi near the venue. Detectives are urging anyone who saw the car or a disturbance nearby to get in touch. It is believed the vehicle was involved in the fatal incident

Past cases similar to the divided council suggest steep legal costs and £1,200-a-day commissioner fees could lie ahead if a deadlock cannot be broken The political stalemate at Oldham follows the 2026 local elections where Labour lost eight seats leaving the party. Reform UK is now the second largest group behind Labour by two councillors on 16 No group has more than a third of the seats in the chamber, with 31 needed to take control. After the local elections, Labour council leader Arooj Shah announced she would stand down and her party step back and Reform ruled out forming any coalition There is currently no one running the council and there are fears the government could step in. The divisions in Oldham are so severe even the appointment of a ceremonial mayor has been caught in the turmoil With no new mayor, there is no way the council can legally move ahead with business including appointing a new leader and councillors to top positions. Hours of talks on May 20 failed to find any way through and another meeting has now been scheduled for June 15 to elect a new ma

evidence_packet.evidence_text: News Greater Manchester News Police remain at scene of 'concern for welfare' at Bury The Rock car park after 20 hours Emergency crews and specialist negotiators were first called to the car park on Tuesday (May 26) News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 20:22, 27 Police and other emergency services remain at the scene of a 'concern for welfare' incident at the Bury The Rock car park after amid a 20-hour stand off Officers, along with fire crews and paramedics, were called to the incident at the junction with Rochdale Road and Derby Way at around 11pm on Tuesday (May 26) As of 7.30pm on Wednesday, the scene remained active with specialist negotiators in attendance. Multiple police vehicles could be seen parked up on the ground with a small cordon put in place It's understood the incident involved a woman on the top floor of the car park complex. The incident comes just days after police and negotiators again spent 24 hours at the car park dealing with a concern for welfare incident, then cordoning the area off for a further 11 hours d View 3 Images Police in attendance (Image: Low Cost Removals of Bury / Tommy Heap)

evidence_packet.lead: News Greater Manchester News Chorlton A ‘thriving new destination’ on way as wrecking ball to swing at Chorlton Cross Shopping Centre It will bring major changes to the area Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 06:20, 27 May 2026 View Image How the scheme could loo
evidence_packet.summary: News Greater Manchester News Chorlton A ‘thriving new destination’ on way as wrecking ball to swing at Chorlton Cross Shopping Centre It will bring major changes to the area Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 06:20, 27 May 2026 View Image How the scheme could loo
evidence_packet.evidence_text: News Greater Manchester News Chorlton A ‘thriving new destination’ on way as wrecking ball to swing at Chorlton Cross Shopping Centre It will bring major changes to the area Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 06:20, 27 May 2026 View Image How the scheme could loo An area in Chorlton is about to be turned into a ‘thriving new destination’ with more homes and public spaces on the way. Plans are in place by PJ Livesey to redevelop the former Chorlton Cross Shopping Centre on Wilbraham Road in a major regeneration scheme The project was granted planning permission at the end of 2025. That work is due to start with phase one focused around dismantling the former shopping centre and Graeme House buildings Bosses behind the scheme say the first step will be to close the precinct car park at the end May 2026. New hoardings will be put up around the site, and the cut through via Manchester Road to Nicolas Road will be closed to pedestrians and vehicles for safety reasons Demolition of the existing buildings is expected to begin in June and finish in August. Material from the existing buildings are set to be reused during construction of the new neighbourhood, to reduce vehicle trips during the work The approved plans include building 262 apartments with a mix of one, two and three bedrooms, all with access to outdoor space through balconies and gardens

With thousands of performances across 50+ countries, they carry the living spirit of Sufi shrine music to global audiences, offering a rar Led by Tahir Qawwal and supported by a masterful ensemble of tabla, harmonium, and choral vocals, the group stays rooted in the classical Qawwali tradition-without fusion, electronics, or pop crossover. Each performance is a vibrant expression of spiritual passion, featuring the The atmosphere includes whirling Sufi dancing and a communal, ecstatic energy that transcends language or belief. Though born in the Sufi Islamic tradition, this music invites all people–regardless of background-into a shared space of unity and divine love. For those new to Qawwa Join us for an unforgettable evening of music that uplifts the heart and honours the timeless message of the Sufis VIP £79.20 * Full Price £38.50 Early Bird £31.00 (available until Sat 3 Jan 2026)
